steps.title: Identify the opening phrase
steps.content: The sentence begins with an introductory participial phrase: Powered with energy collected by solar panels during the day. That phrase modifies the main clause about the blinking LEDs keeping lions away.

steps.title: Place one boundary comma
steps.content: Choice A inserts internal commas around a restrictive phrase and omits the needed final comma. Choice B keeps the phrase intact but omits the comma before the main clause. Choice D wrongly separates energy from the phrase identifying that energy. The correct punctuation uses one comma after the introductory phrase. The answer is C.
